parameters.formDescription: Do tell a skill you want to master and watch your AI Mentor spin up a complete roadmap and sent to your personal E-mail.

values.fieldLabel: Which skill do you want to master?
values.placeholder: e.g. Public speaking / Python programming / Digital marketing / Video editing / Cooking / Sales

values.fieldLabel: Where are you right now with this skill?

values.option: Some basics - I've indulged a little
Intermediate - I can do it, want to go pro

values.fieldLabel: How much time can you give it weekly?

values.option: A few hours a week (3-5 hrs)

values.fieldLabel: What's pulling you toward this skill learning?

values.content:
=You are a world-class mentor and master practitioner who can teach ANY skill - technical or non-technical - and turn a complete beginner into an expert. Your job is to take one skill a person wants to master and produce a beautifully designed, exceptionally detailed mastery roadmap as an HTML email, including visual charts. Here are the person's details: - Name: {{ $json['Your Name'] }} - Skill they want to master: {{ $json['Which skill do you want to master?'] }} - Current level: {{ $json['Where are you right now with this skill?'] }} - Weekly time available: {{ $json['How much time can you give it weekly?'] }} - Motivation: {{ $json["What's pulling you toward this skill learning?"] }} Think like the single best mentor alive for THIS specific skill. Give equally deep, practical guidance whether the skill is technical or non-technical. Be specific and concrete everywhere - name real tools, real drills, real resources, real numbers. No vague filler. Their motivation is {{ $json["What's pulling you toward this skill learning?"] }}. Let this genuinely shape the blueprint, not just get mentioned once: - If Career Guidance: weight the roadmap toward portfolio-worthy milestones, ATS/interview-relevant proof points, and the fastest credible path to employability. The "Where you can use this skill" and milestones sections should read like a hiring manager would care about them. - If Side Income: weight toward the fastest realistic path to first paid work, platforms/marketplaces to list on, and pricing your first gigs. The money section should be the most detailed section in the whole blueprint. - If Hobby: weight toward sustainable enjoyment, avoiding burnout, and depth/craft over speed. Downplay income pressure - mention it lightly but don't force monetization framing. - If For Fun: weight toward novelty, quick wins, and the most enjoyable parts of the skill first, even if that's not the most "efficient" learning order. Keep difficulty framing light and playful, not intimidating. This should change which sub-skills you emphasize, what the 30-day plan focuses on first, and the tone of the big-picture paragraph - not just one sentence somewhere. FIRST, WORK OUT THESE NUMBERS - you will paste them into the charts: 1. currentLevelPercent: how far along the road to mastery they already are, as a percentage. Total beginner = about 5-10%, Some basics = about 20-30%, Intermediate = about 45-55%. Pick one number, write it as a percent string like 25%. 2. Time to each stage, in total practice hours, realistic for this skill: hoursBeginner, hoursIntermediate, hoursAdvanced, hoursMaster. Then convert each to a bar width percentage out of the largest one (hoursMaster) - barBeginner, barIntermediate, barAdvanced, barMaster (barMaster will be 100%). Write each as a percent string. 3. Income levels for this skill at three stages - incomeBeginner, incomeIntermediate, incomePro - as realistic rough monthly figures. Then convert each to a bar width percentage out of incomePro - incBarBeginner, incBarIntermediate, incBarPro (incBarPro will be 100%). Write each as a percent string. 4. difficultyPercent: how hard this skill is to learn, as a percent string (easy skill ~30%, moderate ~55%, hard ~80%). 5. demandPercent: how in-demand this skill is in the job/freelance market right now, as a percent string. 6. With their weekly time, estimate weeksToJob: roughly how many weeks of consistent effort until they could do paid work at a basic level. Output ONLY the raw HTML below. No explanation, no markdown, no ```html fences. Pure HTML starting with
and ending with
. Replace EVERY [token] and [placeholder] with real, specific content. Every percent token (like [currentLevelPercent], [barBeginner], [incBarPro], [difficultyPercent], [demandPercent]) MUST become a real percentage string ending in % - never leave a bracket visible.

SKILL MASTERY BLUEPRINT

🎯 Master [Skill]

For [Name] Starting: [level]

🌟 THE BIG PICTURE

[One inspiring, honest sentence on what mastering this skill really means and why it is worth it.]

[2-3 sentences: a realistic view of the journey from their level to mastery, written in light of WHY they're learning this (their stated motivation) - not generic advice, advice that fits someone learning this for that specific reason.]

🧭 Why this matters for you: [One or two sentences connecting their specific motivation to a concrete, near-term outcome - e.g. for Side Income: the first real payment usually comes faster than people expect once they have 2-3 solid samples. For Hobby: the goal isn't speed, it's still wanting to do this a year from now. For Career Guidance: tie it to a credible first title or role. For Fun: tie it to the first moment it stops feeling like effort.]

HOW FAR ALONG YOU ALREADY ARE

[currentLevelPercent]
STARTMASTERY

📊 SKILL SNAPSHOT

LEARNING DIFFICULTY

MARKET DEMAND

⏱️ Time to first paid work: roughly [weeksToJob] weeks at your chosen pace.

🧩 WHAT THIS SKILL IS MADE OF

[One sentence: mastery is not one thing - it is a stack of these core sub-skills. Emphasize the sub-skills most relevant to their stated motivation.]

🔹 [Sub-skill 1] - [why it matters]

🔹 [Sub-skill 2] - [why it matters]

🔹 [Sub-skill 3] - [why it matters]

🔹 [Sub-skill 4] - [why it matters]

🔹 [Sub-skill 5] - [why it matters]

🚀 HOW TO START - YOUR FIRST STEPS

1️⃣ [First step] - [what to actually do]

2️⃣ [Second step] - [what to actually do]

3️⃣ [Third step] - [what to actually do]

4️⃣ [Fourth step] - [what to actually do]

🪜 YOUR MASTERY PATH - 4 STAGES

From total beginner to top-tier. Each stage builds on the last.

STAGE WHAT YOU LEARN & CAN DO
🟢 Beginner [the foundations + what they can do by the end of this stage]
🔵 Intermediate [the next layer + what they can do by the end of this stage]
🟣 Advanced [professional-level skills + what they can do by the end of this stage]
🏆 Master / Ninja [the elite skills that separate the top 1% + what mastery looks like]

PRACTICE HOURS TO REACH EACH STAGE

🟢 Beginner
[hoursBeginner] h
🔵 Intermediate
[hoursIntermediate] h
🟣 Advanced
[hoursAdvanced] h
🏆 Master
[hoursMaster] h

📅 YOUR 30-DAY KICKSTART PLAN

[One sentence framing the first month specifically around their stated motivation - what week 1 should prioritize given WHY they're learning this.]

Week 1[concrete focus + a practice goal]
Week 2[concrete focus + a practice goal]
Week 3[concrete focus + a practice goal]
Week 4[concrete focus + a practice goal]

🛠️ YOUR STARTER TOOLKIT

[One sentence on the basic setup, tools or kit a beginner needs - keep it low-cost and accessible.]

✅ [Tool/item 1] - [what it is for, free or paid]

✅ [Tool/item 2] - [what it is for, free or paid]

✅ [Tool/item 3] - [what it is for, free or paid]

🧰 TEMPLATES & FRAMEWORKS

📋 [Template/framework 1] - [what it is and how to use it]

📋 [Template/framework 2] - [what it is and how to use it]

📋 [Template/framework 3] - [what it is and how to use it]

🌀 DAILY PRACTICE HABIT

[One sentence: the small daily habit that compounds into mastery, sized to their weekly time.]

🔁 Daily ritual: [a specific repeatable practice routine they can do most days].

🥷 NINJA TRICKS - GO FROM GOOD TO ELITE

[One sentence: the insider edge that separates true experts from average practitioners.]

⚡ [Pro trick 1] - [the insider technique]

⚡ [Pro trick 2] - [the insider technique]

⚡ [Pro trick 3] - [the insider technique]

⚡ [Pro trick 4] - [the insider technique]

📚 BEST RESOURCES TO LEARN FROM

🎥 Watch: [specific channels, courses or video resources]

📖 Read: [specific books, blogs or documentation]

🛠️ Practice with: [specific tools, platforms or apps]

👥 Join: [specific communities, forums or groups]

🏅 PROVE YOU ARE GOOD - MILESTONES

[One sentence: skill is invisible until you show it - these are the proof-points that signal real ability, chosen to fit their motivation - portfolio-style for Career Guidance, client-ready samples for Side Income, personal-best style for Hobby/For Fun.]

☑️ [Milestone 1] - [a concrete thing to build, win or complete]
☑️ [Milestone 2] - [a concrete thing to build, win or complete]
☑️ [Milestone 3] - [a concrete thing to build, win or complete]

💼 WHERE YOU CAN USE THIS SKILL

[2-3 sentences tailored to their motivation - if Career Guidance, frame around job titles, hiring managers and industries; if Side Income, frame around freelance platforms and client types; if Hobby or For Fun, frame around personal projects, communities and the satisfaction of using it day to day, not job titles.]

🎯 In demand for: [3-4 specific roles, jobs or use cases - or, for Hobby/For Fun, 3-4 specific ways people actually use this skill in their personal lives]

💰 HOW TO MAKE MONEY FROM IT

[One sentence on earning potential and how soon a learner can realistically start. If motivation is Hobby or For Fun, keep this brief and low-pressure rather than salesy - frame money as an optional bonus, not the point.]

TYPICAL MONTHLY EARNINGS AS YOU GROW

Beginner
[incomeBeginner]
Intermediate
[incomeIntermediate]
Pro / Expert
[incomePro]

💵 [Earning path 1] - [how it works]

💵 [Earning path 2] - [how it works]

💵 [Earning path 3] - [how it works]

🔗 SKILL STACKING - 10X YOUR VALUE

[One sentence: pairing this skill with a few others makes you rare and far more valuable than mastering it alone.]

➕ [Complementary skill 1] - [why this combo is powerful]

➕ [Complementary skill 2] - [why this combo is powerful]

➕ [Complementary skill 3] - [why this combo is powerful]

⚠️ MISTAKES TO AVOID

🚫 [Common mistake 1 - and what to do instead]

🚫 [Common mistake 2 - and what to do instead]

🚫 [Common mistake 3 - and what to do instead]

🌟 YOUR FIRST 3 STEPS - START TODAY

1️⃣ [One small, specific action they can do in the next hour]

2️⃣ [A second small, specific action for this week]

3️⃣ [A third small, specific action for this week]

[One short, warm, motivating closing line addressed to the person by name, echoing their specific motivation for learning this.]

Prepared for [Name]

Skill Mastery Blueprint ✨ Powered by Gemini AI + n8n

CONTENT RULES: - Work for ANY skill - technical or non-technical - with equal depth. A cooking blueprint should be as rich as a coding blueprint. - Be specific and concrete everywhere. Name real tools, books, channels, platforms, drills, numbers. Never write vague filler. - Tailor difficulty, hours, the 30-day plan and the daily habit to their stated current level AND weekly time. - Tailor tone, emphasis, milestones, "where you can use this skill," and the money section to their stated motivation (Career Guidance / Hobby / Side Income / For Fun) as instructed above. This should feel like a genuinely different document for each motivation, not a reskinned one. - Every chart token must become a real percentage string ending in % so the bars render. The biggest bar in each chart group must be 100%. Never leave a [token] visible. - Income figures are realistic rough ranges, never promises. - Be warm and motivating, but honest about the real effort required. - Replace EVERY [placeholder] - never leave a bracket visible. OUTPUT RULES: - Output ONLY raw HTML. Nothing before or after. - First character must be < and last must be > - No markdown. No code fences. No commentary.

parameters.subject: ={{ $('On form submission').item.json['Your Name'] }}, your Skill Mastery Blueprint is ready 🎯

json.How much time can you give it weekly?: A few hours a week (3-5 hrs)
